_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I have a complete understanding of the question and am ready to summarize.

CSS files receive cache-busting hashes via Hugo's `fingerprint` pipe. The main JavaScript bundle also gets hashed. However, `theme-init.js` is served from `static/js/` with a hardcoded path and lacks any cache-busting mechanism.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Yes — for the main CSS and JS bundles, but not for every asset.

## Fingerprinting (browser cache busting)

**CSS** — Hugo fingerprints the Tailwind output:

```1:8:layouts/partials/css.html
{{ with resources.Get "css/main.css" }}
  {{ $opts := dict "minify" (not hugo.IsDevelopment) }}
  {{ with . | css.TailwindCSS $opts | fingerprint }}
    <link
      rel="stylesheet"
      href="{{ .RelPermalink }}"
      {{ if not hugo.IsDevelopment }}integrity="{{ .Data.Integrity }}" crossorigin="anonymous"{{ end }}
    />
```

**JS** — The concatenated bundle (`theme.js`, `nav.js`, `search.js`, `recent.js`) is also fingerprinted:

```12:16:layouts/_default/baseof.html
    {{ $js := slice (resources.Get "js/theme.js") (resources.Get "js/nav.js") (resources.Get "js/search.js") (resources.Get "js/recent.js") | resources.Concat "js/site.js" | minify | fingerprint }}
    <script
      src="{{ $js.RelPermalink }}"
      {{ if not hugo.IsDevelopment }}integrity="{{ $js.Data.Integrity }}" crossorigin="anonymous"{{ end }}
    ></script>
```

`fingerprint` adds a content hash to the filename (e.g. `/css/main.abc123def.css`, `/js/site.xyz789.js`). When the file changes, the URL changes, so browsers fetch the new version.

## Hugo build cache busters

`hugo.toml` also has **build-time** cache busters so Hugo rebuilds CSS when Tailwind-related inputs change:

```25:31:hugo.toml
  [[build.cachebusters]]
    source = 'assets/notwatching/hugo_stats\.json'
    target = 'css'

  [[build.cachebusters]]
    source = '(postcss|tailwind)\.config\.js'
    target = 'css'
```

Those affect Hugo’s internal build cache, not the URL the browser sees.

## Long-lived HTTP caching

`netlify.toml` and `static/_headers` set `/css/*` and `/js/*` to `max-age=31536000, immutable`. That’s safe because fingerprinted assets get new paths when content changes.

## Exception: `theme-init.js`

This one is **not** fingerprinted — it’s a static file with a fixed path:

```2:2:layouts/partials/head.html
  <script src="/js/theme-init.js"></script>
```

It lives in `static/js/theme-init.js`, so it keeps the same URL across deploys unless you change the filename or add versioning yourself.

**Summary:** Main CSS and the site JS bundle use Hugo fingerprinting. `theme-init.js` does not. If you want that busted too, you’d need to move it into `assets/` and pipe it through `resources.Get` + `fingerprint`, or add a manual query string/version.

---

_**User**_

proceed

---

**Changes:**
- Moved `static/js/theme-init.js` → `assets/js/theme-init.js`
- Updated `head.html` to load it via `resources.Get` → `minify` → `fingerprint`

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Mobile header buttons need right alignment.

The header flex layout leaves the buttons centered rather than right-aligned on mobile. Adding `ml-auto` or wrapping the buttons in a flex container should push them to the right edge.

Adding `ml-auto` directly to the theme toggle risks breaking the desktop layout. On larger screens, the search field uses `mx-auto flex-1`, so pushing the toggle right could misalign the flex row. A wrapper container may be safer than targeting the button directly.

A wrapper div with `ml-auto flex items-center gap-2` around both buttons is the better approach. This pushes them right on mobile without affecting the desktop flex layout.

Reconsidering the wrapper approach: on desktop, `ml-auto` on the button group would push it to the far right while the search field expands centrally. This may actually be the desired layout.

Applying `ml-auto` only below the medium breakpoint—`ml-auto md:ml-0`—would keep the desktop layout intact while forcing the buttons to the right edge on mobile.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

The theme toggle and menu button are wrapped in a flex group with `ml-auto`, so on mobile they sit on the right while the title stays on the left.

Layout on small screens: `[Title ············] [🌙] [☰]`

The same grouping keeps those controls pinned to the right on larger breakpoints too, without affecting the desktop search or nav layout.
